title: Consolida teheranica
taxonomy: Consolida teheranica is classified within the plant family Ranunculaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Consolida, which is a group of annual plants commonly known as larkspurs. The genus Consolida is further divided into several species, with Consolida teheranica being one of them. These plants are native to various regions, including Iran, where Consolida teheranica is specifically found. The classification of Consolida teheranica reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Consolida teheranica, also known as Tehran larkspur, is a flowering plant species belonging to the genus Consolida in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, which may aid in treating various conditions including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Consolida teheranica include alkaloids like teheranidine, teheranin, and protopine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Consolida teheranica is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and as a natural remedy for stress relief.
